title: SQL: When NULL is in a column,it shouldn't add primary key
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE B (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, test INTEGER, name TEXT);
INSERT INTO B (test, name) VALUES (NULL, 'Test Name');
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX test_unique ON B(test);
INSERT INTO B (test, name) VALUES (1, 'Another Name');
CREATE TABLE B_temp AS SELECT * FROM B WHERE 1=0;
INSERT INTO B_temp SELECT * FROM B;
key_bug_features: NULL values in primary key column
